mapper.select: select count(*) from market_inventory_unit where listing_id = #{listingId, jdbcType=BINARY} and status = 'AVAILABLE'

select inventory_unit_id, listing_id, seller_user_id, payload_type, payload_content, status, reserved_order_id, delivered_at, create_time from market_inventory_unit where listing_id = #{listingId, jdbcType=BINARY} and status = 'AVAILABLE' order by inventory_unit_id asc limit #{limit} for update

select inventory_unit_id, listing_id, seller_user_id, payload_type, payload_content, status, reserved_order_id, delivered_at, create_time from market_inventory_unit where reserved_order_id = #{reservedOrderId, jdbcType=BINARY} order by inventory_unit_id asc

select inventory_unit_id, listing_id, seller_user_id, payload_type, payload_content, status, reserved_order_id, delivered_at, create_time from market_inventory_unit where listing_id = #{listingId, jdbcType=BINARY} order by inventory_unit_id asc

select inventory_unit_id, listing_id, seller_user_id, payload_type, payload_content, status, reserved_order_id, delivered_at, create_time from market_inventory_unit where inventory_unit_id = #{inventoryUnitId, jdbcType=BINARY}
mapper.update: update market_inventory_unit set status = 'AVAILABLE', reserved_order_id = null where reserved_order_id = #{reservedOrderId, jdbcType=BINARY} and status = 'RESERVED'
update market_inventory_unit set status = 'AVAILABLE', reserved_order_id = null where reserved_order_id = #{orderId, jdbcType=BINARY} and status = 'RESERVED'
